title: Catastrophic admission — multi-system data dump
scenarioBrief: Four months later (2027-03-15), Maria is admitted from clinic with severe weight loss, scaling rash, edema, weakness, and dysphagia. The chart is now flooded: multiple ED visits over the prior 6 weeks, dozens of labs, two outpatient notes, imaging, and consult requests. You are the admitting hospitalist intern. Build a working differential and prioritize the workup. AI assistant available.

chartGate._notes: AP3 visibility: everything through 2027-03-15 admission day — heavy data dump. Inpatient daily progress notes from this admission are NOT yet visible (those land in AP4).

prompts.question: TODO — Phase 2: prompt asking for a prioritized differential given the multi-system picture, with explicit reasoning about what unifies vs splits the findings.

essential: TODO: essential DDx (active SLE flare with myositis, kwashiorkor/severe malnutrition, dysphagia workup needed, infectious considerations given immunosuppression risk)
bonus: TODO: bonus (linking dysphagia → starvation → kwashiorkor causal chain, considering refeeding risk pre-emptively, dermatomyositis vs SLE overlap)
redFlags: TODO: red flags (splitting findings into unrelated buckets, missing nutrition entirely, premature closure on 'SLE flare')

prompts.question: TODO — Phase 2: prompt asking how the resident filtered/summarized the chart for AI given the volume — what they included, excluded, and why.

essential: TODO: essential (weight trajectory, albumin/protein trend, CK if available, recent labs, current meds, dysphagia onset timing)
bonus: TODO: bonus (structured summary rather than raw paste, explicit signal/noise reasoning, anchoring the AI on the time course)
redFlags: TODO: red flags (dumping whole chart, omitting nutritional labs, ignoring social context relevant to access barriers)

prompts.question: TODO — Phase 2: prompt asking for the first 24-hour workup + management plan, including what to order and what to hold.

essential: TODO: essential (CK, aldolase, EMG/muscle biopsy plan, dysphagia eval/swallow study, nutrition consult, electrolytes for refeeding, ID screen before steroids)
bonus: TODO: bonus (cautious feeding initiation, phos/mag/K monitoring schedule, TB reactivation check before immunosuppression)
redFlags: TODO: red flags (high-dose feeding without refeeding precautions, pulse steroids without infection workup, ignoring dysphagia aspiration risk)
